tasks.title: Implement: Session Discovery
tasks.description: Implement and verify the Session Discovery feature from @session-discovery: discover previous sessions from Claude, OpenCode, and other supported backends so users can resume work reliably.
tasks.closed_reason: Integrated first Codex batch; validation passed: session_manager tests, database tests, TokenBurnHistory Vitest, cargo test --lib, git diff --check, bun run build.

notes.author: @claude
notes.content:
Approach: session-discovery.ts already implemented with Claude + OpenCode backends. Need to:
1. Write comprehensive tests for discoverClaudeSessions and discoverOpenCodeSessions
2. Verify edge cases (missing dirs, corrupt files, legacy encoding)
3. Test the dispatch function routes correctly by backend type
Existing code reads ~/.claude/projects/ JSONL files and .opencode/opencode.db SQLite.

tasks.title: Implement: Live Token/Cost HUD
tasks.description: Implement and verify @01KPNWTQ: real-time UI showing current session token burn, cost, and budget status.
tasks.closed_reason: Integrated second Codex batch; validation passed: health_manager tests, gsd_engine tests, HealthDashboard/TokenBudgetHud/useTokenMeter Vitest, cargo test --lib, git diff --check, bun run build.

notes.author: @claude
notes.content:
Approach: 
1. Main process IPC handler reads Claude JSONL session files, sums usage fields
2. Preload bridge exposes getSessionTokenUsage  
3. Api interface + ElectronBackend implementation
4. TokenCostHUD component in terminal header, polls every 10s
5. Supports Claude sessions only (JSONL format has usage data)
Edge cases: no session yet, session file not found, parse errors in JSONL
Test plan: unit test for token parsing logic, component render test

tasks.title: Implement: Token Burn History
tasks.description: Implement and verify @01KPNWTT: SQLite-backed logging and historical analysis of token transactions.
tasks.closed_reason: Integrated first Codex batch; validation passed: session_manager tests, database tests, TokenBurnHistory Vitest, cargo test --lib, git diff --check, bun run build.

notes.author: @claude
notes.content:
Approach: 
1. Create SQLite-backed token-burn-db.ts in src/main/ using better-sqlite3 (already a dep)
2. Create IPC handlers for logging and querying token history
3. Wire preload bridge for renderer access
4. Create TokenBurnHistory React component with table view and aggregation
5. Integrate into OrchestrationPanel

Key patterns: follow session-discovery.ts for SQLite usage, orchestration-handlers.ts for IPC pattern, OrchestrationPanel.tsx for UI integration.

Test plan: 4 ACs × 1+ test each covering DB init, record insertion, query/filtering, and UI rendering.

notes.author: Johan.P
notes.content: Implemented phase-based execution orchestration in src-tauri/src/gsd_engine: plans now seed execution metadata defaults, phases run through a runtime config resolver, steps execute in waves via JoinSet, and each step has a verifier retry loop before atomic commit. Added structured gsd-execution-event emission plus focused unit tests for wave batching, runtime defaults, and verifier retry decisions. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml passed. cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml gsd_engine --lib still fails on a pre-existing Send error in src/health_manager.rs (MutexGuard across await in health_get_status), so the repo-level test gate is currently blocked outside this task.

notes.author: Johan.P
notes.content: Repo build verification passed: bun run build completed successfully and produced src-tauri/target/release/bundle/deb/simple-code-gui_0.1.0_amd64.deb. The earlier cargo test failure is still isolated to the pre-existing health_manager Send issue, not the GSD engine patch.

items.title: Terminal Management
items.description: Core PTY session management, multi-backend CLI support, and terminal lifecycle

items.title: PTY Session Lifecycle
items.description: Spawn, manage, resize, and terminate PTY processes running AI CLI backends. Ring buffer captures last 200 lines with ANSI stripping.

acceptance_criteria.then: a PTY process is created with node-pty using the resolved backend executable, correct cwd, and xterm-256color terminal type
output is captured in a 200-line ring buffer with ANSI codes stripped and relayed to renderer via IPC

acceptance_criteria.when: the user closes the tab or app exits
acceptance_criteria.then: the PTY process is terminated and all listeners are cleaned up

items.title: Multi-Backend Support
items.description: Support multiple AI CLI backends (Claude, Gemini, Codex, OpenCode, Aider) with backend-specific argument mapping for permissions, session resumption, and tool acceptance.

acceptance_criteria.given: a project has backend set to gemini
acceptance_criteria.then: the gemini CLI executable is located and launched with Gemini-specific flags for approval mode and allowed tools

acceptance_criteria.given: a backend has permission mode configured
acceptance_criteria.then: backend-specific CLI flags are applied: Claude uses --permission-mode, Gemini uses --approval-mode, Codex uses --full-auto, Aider uses --yes

items.title: Session Discovery
items.description: Discover previous sessions from Claude (~/.claude/projects/), OpenCode (.opencode/opencode.db via SQLite), and other backends to enable session resumption.

acceptance_criteria.given: existing CLI sessions in standard locations (e.g. ~/.claude/projects)
acceptance_criteria.then: session metadata is extracted and displayed in the project UI

items.title: Runtime Backend Switching
items.description: Switch the AI backend of a running terminal session without closing the tile. Kills old PTY, spawns new one with same cwd, preserves session ID when backends match.

acceptance_criteria.when: the user selects a different AI backend
acceptance_criteria.then: the current PTY is terminated and a new PTY for the selected backend is spawned in the same working directory

items.title: CLI Tool Installation
items.description: Check, install, and manage AI CLI tools (Claude, Gemini, Codex, OpenCode, Aider) with portable Node.js/Python support for offline-first workflows.

acceptance_criteria.given: a missing or outdated AI CLI tool
acceptance_criteria.then: the tool can be installed or updated using the internal package manager logic

items.title: Context Bridge Isolation
items.description: All main process capabilities exposed to renderer through Electron contextBridge. No direct ipcRenderer access in renderer code.

acceptance_criteria.given: the application is running in Electron
acceptance_criteria.when: the renderer attempts to access main process modules
acceptance_criteria.then: only functions exposed through contextBridge are available, with no direct access to ipcRenderer or Node.js built-ins

items.title: Shell Injection Prevention
items.description: All subprocess spawning uses spawn() with argument arrays, never exec() with string interpolation. Task IDs validated with strict regex.

acceptance_criteria.given: a shell command string with untrusted input
acceptance_criteria.then: arguments are passed as an array to prevent shell interpretation of metacharacters

items.title: Layout & Navigation
items.description: Tiled and tabbed terminal layout, sidebar navigation, project management UI

items.title: Tiled Terminal Layout
items.description: Flexible grid layout where terminals are positioned as tiles using percentage-based coordinates. Supports splitting, swapping, and multi-tab tiles on a pannable canvas.

acceptance_criteria.given: user is in tiled view mode
acceptance_criteria.then: a new tile is added to the layout with percentage-based coordinates and the terminal renders inside it

acceptance_criteria.given: multiple tiles exist on the canvas
acceptance_criteria.then: the tile divides into two tiles in the specified direction (top/bottom/left/right) each getting half the space

items.title: Tile Drag & Drop
items.description: Drag projects from sidebar or tabs between tiles. Drop zones support swap, split-top/bottom/left/right. Visual preview during drag.

acceptance_criteria.when: a tile is dragged to a new position
acceptance_criteria.then: the layout updates to reflect the new order

items.title: Tile Resizing
items.description: Resize tiles by dragging edges. Proportional resizing spreads width/height among adjacent tiles. Visual edge highlighting on hover.

acceptance_criteria.then: the adjacent tiles resize proportionally

items.title: Canvas Panning
items.description: Middle-mouse-button horizontal panning across a virtual canvas wider than the viewport. 3px threshold before drag starts, suppresses paste during panning.

acceptance_criteria.then: the view shifts smoothly with no rendering artifacts

items.title: Sidebar Navigation
items.description: Project tree with categories, session lists, inline rename, drag-to-reorder, context menus. Desktop: collapsible left panel with resize handle. Mobile: drawer overlay with hamburger toggle.

acceptance_criteria.then: the corresponding panels open or close with smooth transitions

items.title: Tabbed View Mode
items.description: Traditional tab-based terminal switching as alternative to tiled layout. Supports undo close tab.

acceptance_criteria.then: sessions are organized into tabs instead of tiles

items.title: Advanced Workspace Control
items.description: Enhanced project switcher and home screen with usage summaries and alerts.

acceptance_criteria.then: project switching and organization is fast and intuitive

items.title: Project Quick-Switcher
items.description: Global hotkey or UI element to jump between recent workspaces.

acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Home Usage Summary
items.description: Dashboard view of total token usage and activity across all projects.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Home Usage Summary is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Voice I/O
items.description: Speech-to-text input (Whisper) for hands-free terminal interaction.

items.title: Speech-to-Text Input
items.description: WebRTC audio capture with Whisper.js models (tiny to large-v3). Silence detection for auto-submit, live transcription display, configurable microphone and silence threshold.

acceptance_criteria.given: whisper model is loaded and microphone is active
acceptance_criteria.when: the user speaks and then pauses beyond the silence threshold
acceptance_criteria.then: the audio is transcribed and the text is auto-submitted to the active terminal

items.title: Task Management
items.description: Integrated task tracking via Beads and Kspec backends with unified adapter pattern

items.title: Beads Task Backend
items.description: Task CRUD via Beads CLI (bd command). File watchers on .beads/ directory with 500ms debounce. Task ID validation, shell injection prevention via spawn().

acceptance_criteria.then: the adapter correctly interface with the Beads issue tracker

items.title: Kspec Task Backend
items.description: Spec-driven task management via kspec CLI. Daemon lifecycle management, auto-injection of task instructions into CLAUDE.md, dispatch for autonomous agents.

acceptance_criteria.then: the adapter correctly interfaces with the Kspec spec and task system

items.title: Unified Task Adapter
items.description: Adapter pattern abstracting Beads and Kspec backends behind a unified TaskAdapter interface. Auto-detection of initialized backend, transparent routing, migration support.

acceptance_criteria.then: the kspec adapter is auto-detected and used for all task operations transparently
tasks are migrated from beads to kspec format and the adapter switches to kspec backend

items.title: Task Panel UI
items.description: Collapsible bottom panel showing task list with filtering, sorting, inline editing, status cycling, create/detail modals, and resizable height. State machine: loading → not_installed → not_initialized → ready.

acceptance_criteria.then: tasks are listed with status, priority, and links to specs

items.title: Advanced Orchestration UI
items.description: High-fidelity dashboard for agent status, activity feeds, and approval workflows.

acceptance_criteria.then: the phase tree and execution state are visualized in real-time

items.title: Real-time Activity Feed
items.description: Visual log of all system and agent activities across the current session.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Real-time Activity Feed is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Agent Status Cards
items.description: Detailed status display for individual agents (Planner, Builder, Reviewer).

acceptance_criteria.given: the Agent Status Cards is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Approval Workflow UI
items.description: Interactive prompts for user review of proposed agent modifications.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Approval Workflow UI is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Task Assignment View
items.description: Management view for assigning agents to specific repositories or tasks.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Task Assignment View is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Connectivity
items.description: HTTP API server for prompt injection and external control.

items.title: HTTP Prompt API
items.description: Per-project HTTP server for external prompt injection. Session modes: existing (reuse terminal), new-keep (spawn and keep), new-close (spawn and auto-close). Rate limited to 10 req/60s per IP.

acceptance_criteria.when: an external request is made to the project-specific endpoint
acceptance_criteria.then: the prompt is injected into the target terminal session according to the specified mode

items.title: Extensions & Configuration
items.description: Extension/MCP registry, installation, per-project configuration, settings management, and theming

items.title: Extension Registry
items.description: Fetch, browse, and install extensions (skills, MCPs, agents) from registry or custom URLs. Global and per-project extension scoping with enable/disable controls.

acceptance_criteria.then: it is validated and added to the system registry

items.title: Settings & Theming
items.description: Global and per-project settings: default directory, theme with custom colors, auto-accept tools, permission modes, backend selection. Persisted to JSON with atomic writes and backup.

acceptance_criteria.then: the UI updates immediately to reflect the new theme

items.title: Workspace Persistence
items.description: Persist projects, open tabs, tile layout, categories, and view mode to workspace.json. Atomic saves with temp file + rename, backup restoration, and data migration from old app names.

acceptance_criteria.then: data is written atomically via temp file + rename with backup, and validated before finalizing

items.title: Auto-Update
items.description: Check for, download, and install app updates via electron-updater. Status events relayed to renderer for progress UI.

acceptance_criteria.then: the app downloads and prepares the update in the background

items.title: System Operational Telemetry
items.description: Operational monitoring for app health, crash reporting, and background jobs.

acceptance_criteria.then: operational telemetry is collected and reported

items.title: App & Plugin Health Dashboard
items.description: Status monitoring for core services and installed MCP/Plugins.

acceptance_criteria.given: the App & Plugin Health Dashboard is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Background Jobs Visibility
items.description: Panel to monitor running background processes (scans, indices, updates).

acceptance_criteria.given: the Background Jobs Visibility is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: AI Intelligence & Orchestration
items.description: Everything related to model providers, agent execution, and repository intelligence.

items.title: Multi-Provider AI Runtime
items.description: Abstraction layer and routing for Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and Ollama providers.

acceptance_criteria.then: the runtime selects the optimal provider based on cost and capability

items.title: Provider Abstraction Interface
items.description: Unified trait/interface for different LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama).

acceptance_criteria.given: the Provider Abstraction Interface is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Auth & API Key Management
items.description: Secure storage and retrieval of provider credentials in SQLite.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Auth & API Key Management is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Model Routing Policies
items.description: System for selecting models based on task type (planning, patching, summarization).

acceptance_criteria.given: the Model Routing Policies is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Named Model Plans
items.description: User-definable presets for cost/quality balance (e.g., Budget, High-Quality).

acceptance_criteria.given: the Named Model Plans is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: GSD Execution Engine
items.description: Phase-based agent execution engine with verifier loops and wave-based orchestration.

acceptance_criteria.then: it follows the phase sequence with verification loops and wave orchestration

items.title: Phase-based Decomposition
items.description: Logic to break large tasks into sequential, manageable phases.

acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Wave-based Execution
items.description: Orchestrator for executing task waves with dependency awareness.

acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Verification & Retry Loop
items.description: Automated quality checks and retry logic for failed agent actions.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Verification & Retry Loop is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Execution State Persistence
items.description: Tracking and resuming long-running agent sessions across app restarts.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Execution State Persistence is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Token Economics & Telemetry
items.description: Real-time tracking of token consumption and cost savings from RTK/MCP/GitNexus.

acceptance_criteria.given: A terminal session is running an AI backend (Claude, Gemini, etc.)
acceptance_criteria.when: Token usage data appears in terminal output
acceptance_criteria.then: The system parses and stores token counts per session in the telemetry store

acceptance_criteria.given: Token data has been collected for one or more sessions
acceptance_criteria.when: The user views the telemetry panel
acceptance_criteria.then: Session token totals, estimated cost, and cumulative totals are displayed
RTK savings data from rtk gain is displayed alongside raw token usage

acceptance_criteria.when: Token data updates in any session
acceptance_criteria.then: Per-session and aggregate totals update in real-time without page refresh

items.title: Live Token/Cost HUD
items.description: Real-time UI component showing current session burn and budget status.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Live Token/Cost HUD is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Savings Telemetry (RTK/MCP)
items.description: Calculation and display of tokens saved by using RTK, MCP, and local intelligence.

acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Token Burn History
items.description: SQLite-backed logging of all token transactions for historical analysis.

acceptance_criteria.when: The token burn history module initializes
acceptance_criteria.then: A SQLite database is created/opened at the user data directory with a token_transactions table

acceptance_criteria.given: A terminal session produces token usage data (input/output tokens)
acceptance_criteria.when: The token data is received by the main process
acceptance_criteria.then: A transaction record is inserted into SQLite with session_id, project_path, backend, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost_estimate, and timestamp

acceptance_criteria.when: The renderer requests history via IPC
acceptance_criteria.then: Aggregated and per-session token data is returned with filtering by date range, project, and backend

acceptance_criteria.given: The renderer has received token history data
acceptance_criteria.when: The Token Burn History UI component renders
acceptance_criteria.then: A historical view shows token usage over time with charts, totals, and breakdown by project/backend

items.title: Budget & Rate Limits
items.description: Configurable caps on token usage per task or session.

acceptance_criteria.given: the Budget & Rate Limits is implemented
acceptance_criteria.then: it works as expected

items.title: Brainstorm Companion
items.description:
A dedicated ideation and specification workspace integrated into the Project Intelligence Sidebar.
Bridges the gap between raw thoughts (seeds) and formal requirements.

acceptance_criteria.given: The application is running and the Intelligence Sidebar is open
acceptance_criteria.when: The user selects the Brainstorm tab
acceptance_criteria.then: The Brainstorm Companion interface is displayed with Ideas and Drafts sub-tabs

acceptance_criteria.when: The user switches between Ideas and Drafts tabs
acceptance_criteria.then: The UI state is preserved and the corresponding component is rendered

items.title: Idea Inbox (The Foundry)
items.description: A component for quick-capturing and managing "Seeds" (raw ideas).

acceptance_criteria.when: The user enters text in the capture field and presses Enter
acceptance_criteria.then: A new Seed is created via GSD and appears in the list

acceptance_criteria.given: A list of Seeds is displayed
acceptance_criteria.then: The Seed details are shown with promotion options

items.title: Spec Draft Editor
items.description: A structured, visual editor for drafting KSpec modules and acceptance criteria.

acceptance_criteria.when: The user enters spec details (Title, Type, ACs)
acceptance_criteria.then: The editor provides real-time validation and a live preview of the spec structure
The draft is persisted to the .kspec/ shadow branch via the Rust backend

items.title: Promotion Workflow
items.description: Workflow for converting Seeds into Spec Drafts or Beads Tasks.

acceptance_criteria.given: An idea is selected in the Idea Inbox
acceptance_criteria.then: The Spec Draft Editor opens pre-populated with the idea's content
A new Beads issue is created and linked to the corresponding spec if applicable

plans.title: Plan: Brainstorm Companion Implementation
plans.content:
# Plan: Brainstorm Companion Implementation

## Specs

```yaml
- title: Brainstorm Companion
  slug: brainstorm-companion
  type: feature
  parent: "@ai-core"
  description: |
    A dedicated ideation and specification workspace integrated into the Project Intelligence Sidebar.
    Bridges the gap between raw thoughts (seeds) and formal requirements.
  acceptance_criteria:
    - id: ac-1
      given: |
        The application is running and the Intelligence Sidebar is open
      when: |
        The user selects the Brainstorm tab
      then: |
        The Brainstorm Companion interface is displayed with Ideas and Drafts sub-tabs
    - id: ac-2
      given: |
        The Brainstorm Companion is open
      when: |
        The user switches between Ideas and Drafts tabs
      then: |
        The UI state is preserved and the corresponding component is rendered

- title: Idea Inbox (The Foundry)
  slug: idea-inbox
  type: feature
  parent: "@brainstorm-companion"
  description: |
    A component for quick-capturing and managing "Seeds" (raw ideas).
  acceptance_criteria:
    - id: ac-1
      given: |
        The Ideas tab is open
      when: |
        The user enters text in the capture field and presses Enter
      then: |
        A new Seed is created via GSD and appears in the list
    - id: ac-2
      given: |
        A list of Seeds is displayed
      when: |
        The user selects a Seed
      then: |
        The Seed details are shown with promotion options

- title: Spec Draft Editor
  slug: spec-draft-editor
  type: feature
  parent: "@brainstorm-companion"
  description: |
    A structured, visual editor for drafting KSpec modules and acceptance criteria.
  acceptance_criteria:
    - id: ac-1
      given: |
        The Drafts tab is open
      when: |
        The user enters spec details (Title, Type, ACs)
      then: |
        The editor provides real-time validation and a live preview of the spec structure
    - id: ac-2
      given: |
        A draft is in progress
      when: |
        The user saves the draft
      then: |
        The draft is persisted to the .kspec/ shadow branch via the Rust backend

- title: Promotion Workflow
  slug: promotion-workflow
  type: feature
  parent: "@brainstorm-companion"
  description: |
    Workflow for converting Seeds into Spec Drafts or Beads Tasks.
  acceptance_criteria:
    - id: ac-1
      given: |
        An idea is selected in the Idea Inbox
      when: |
        The user clicks "Promote to Spec"
      then: |
        The Spec Draft Editor opens pre-populated with the idea's content
    - id: ac-2
      given: |
        An idea is selected
      when: |
        The user clicks "Promote to Task"
      then: |
        A new Beads issue is created and linked to the corresponding spec if applicable
```

## Tasks

derive_from_specs: true

```yaml
- title: "Frontend: BrainstormTab and Layout"
  slug: task-brainstorm-tab-layout
  description: |
    Create the main BrainstormTab container and implement the sub-tab navigation (Ideas/Drafts).
    Integrate into the IntelligenceSidebar.
  priority: 2
  tags: [frontend, layout]
  spec_ref: "@brainstorm-companion"

- title: "Frontend: IdeaInbox Component"
  slug: task-idea-inbox-frontend
  description: |
    Implement the IdeaInbox component with seed list and capture input.
    Wire up gsdListSeeds IPC calls.
  priority: 2
  tags: [frontend, gsd]
  spec_ref: "@idea-inbox"

- title: "Frontend: SpecDraftEditor Component"
  slug: task-spec-draft-editor-frontend
  description: |
    Implement the structured YAML editor with live preview and validation.
    Wire up kspecWriteDraft IPC calls.
  priority: 2
  tags: [frontend, kspec]
  spec_ref: "@spec-draft-editor"

- title: "Backend: KSpec Draft Persistence"
  slug: task-backend-kspec-draft
  description: |
    Implement the Rust side of kspecWriteDraft to persist drafts into the shadow branch.
  priority: 2
  tags: [backend, rust, kspec]
  spec_ref: "@spec-draft-editor"

- title: "Integration: Promotion IPC Bridge"
  slug: task-promotion-ipc-bridge
  description: |
    Implement the IPC bridge for bdCreateTask and handle the conversion logic between seeds, specs, and tasks.
  priority: 2
  tags: [integration, beads, kspec, gsd]
  spec_ref: "@promotion-workflow"
```

agents.name: Task Worker
agents.description: Autonomous task worker. Picks up automation-eligible ready and needs_work tasks.

agents.name: PR Reviewer
agents.description: Automated PR reviewer. Reviews pending_review tasks and merges when quality gates pass.

agents.name: Primary Development Agent
agents.description: General-purpose development agent for code, testing, refactoring, and code review tasks.

agents.name: Plan Reviewer
agents.description:
Reviews plans for quality, completeness, and spec alignment.
Has review capability and plan-focused skills attached.

Adapter guidance: This agent uses the default adapter configured
for your project. If authentication fails with the default adapter,
you can switch adapters using:
  kspec meta set agents --update plan-reviewer --adapter <adapter-name>
Run `kspec agent adapters` to list available adapters and their
authentication requirements.

rules: Use conventional commit format (feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore)
Reference task in commit body when applicable (Task: @task-slug)
PLACEHOLDER: Replace this rule with your project's architectural constraints and patterns
PLACEHOLDER: Replace this rule with your project's testing expectations and coverage requirements
